title: Iran War economic cover-up — 2026-05-18
content:
The Trump administration asserts the US-Iran conflict is economically manageable and strategically justified. Public statements frame the war as low-cost to Americans. Trump cited inaccurate data to downplay soaring gas prices, rising inflation, and American casualties. Officials claim oil price volatility is temporary and domestic inflation is under control.

## What the Data Says

- IMF slashed global growth forecast to 3.1% (down from 3.3% in January); inflation rising to 4.4%
- IMF severe scenario: 2% global growth, 6%+ inflation if energy disruptions extend into 2027
- Brent Crude surged past $120/barrel after Strait of Hormuz closure (March 4, 2026)
- Global commodity price index up 18%; average global inflation 7.5%
- OECD foreign aid dropped ~25% in 2025 vs 2024
- Climate finance commitments ($300B/yr NCQG, COP29) at risk as defense spending crowds out development
- 15 million displaced; Energy, Agriculture, Manufacturing sectors in chaos
- Internal contradiction: Trump said US might strike Iran's Kharg Island oil hub "just for fun" while claiming oil spikes benefit the US

## The Real Story

The administration is using cherry-picked or inaccurate metrics to sell a war whose real cost — measured in global inflation, displaced millions, climate finance collapse — is being externalized onto ordinary Americans and the Global South. The gas-price-downplay narrative collapses against IMF data, commodity indices, and the administration's own contradictory statements.
